cards.title: Frame-indexed callsite leak primitive
cards.trigger: saved rbp controllable, lea rax [rbp-k], mov rdi rax, call printf puts, original callsite reuse, rbp argument selector
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-primitive-compressor or ctf-pwn-stack-frame-solver, then one minimal rbp=target+k callsite probe
cards.oracle: target bytes or raw GOT/libc pointer bytes printed before crash/EOF
cards.stop_rule: do not build fake-stack libc calls, GOT rewrite loops, or one_gadget closure before this primitive probe
cards.pivot_rule: if original callsite is reached and target is not consumed, then consider synthetic closure or alternate target
cards.snippet: saved rbp selects [rbp-k] argument for original printf/puts callsite

cards.title: Over-reconstructed fake stack before original callsite probe
cards.trigger: fake stack libc crash, stdio corruption, synthetic frame instability while original rbp-indexed callsite untested
cards.first_safe_check: return to primitive compression: rbp=target+k, rip=original callsite
cards.oracle: minimal original callsite probe is confirmed or falsified by real oracle
cards.stop_rule: complex closure failure cannot falsify simpler original callsite primitive
cards.pivot_rule: after two synthetic-environment explanations, rollback to shortest primitive
cards.snippet: do not debug synthetic closure before original callsite primitive
retrieval_intents: fake stack before callsite anti pattern
query_aliases: fake stack printf crash not primitive failure

cards.title: ret2win direct closure
cards.trigger: win symbol, backdoor, print_flag, direct privileged function, no canary or already neutralized
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-crash-probe + one minimal direct ret2win payload
cards.oracle: privileged function lands or direct route is concretely falsified
cards.stop_rule: do not build ret2libc or broader ROP before one direct ret2win attempt
cards.pivot_rule: if arguments, alignment, or base are needed, downgrade to the smallest matching next family

cards.title: ret2libc leak first closure
cards.trigger: NX enabled, libc route likely, puts leak, printf leak, show leak, leak-first closure
cards.first_safe_check: build one leak-only stage and safe return
cards.oracle: one stable classified leak yields sane libc base
cards.stop_rule: do not rotate one_gadget or alternate libc before one stable leak-first stage
cards.pivot_rule: if shell is blocked, promote ORW/file-read closure
cards.snippet: ret2libc leak first before final chain

cards.title: format string leak before write
cards.trigger: printf user, format string, positional leak, fmt offset, write-first drift
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-format-map and one leak-only payload
cards.oracle: stable leak map appears before any write
cards.stop_rule: no %n writes before offset, RELRO, and target writability are known
cards.pivot_rule: if read-only leak already closes, demote write-first ideas

cards.title: no canary no PIE shortest ROP
cards.trigger: no canary, no PIE, simple BOF, shortest ROP, direct ret2win or ret2libc
cards.first_safe_check: prove control then test direct symbol, imported function, then minimal ret2libc
cards.oracle: one minimal chain lands or concrete blocker forces next family
cards.stop_rule: do not open heap, SROP, CSU, or stack-pivot while shortest ROP remains untested
cards.pivot_rule: if direct target is absent and leakless closure is blocked, move to leak-first ret2libc
cards.snippet: no canary no PIE shortest path
query_aliases: no canary no pie shortest rop

cards.title: canary leak first
cards.trigger: canary found, stack overwrite family, stack leak, show leak, format leak
cards.first_safe_check: search leak paths before brute-force or final chain work
cards.oracle: a leak exposes canary or a non-return route becomes shorter
cards.stop_rule: do not brute-force canary without stable fork oracle
cards.pivot_rule: if canary is unnecessary because non-return closure is shorter, promote that route

cards.title: PIE base before gadget
cards.trigger: PIE enabled, binary leak needed, unknown class leak, gadget math before base
cards.first_safe_check: classify leaks and compute base only from known binary/code pointers
cards.oracle: one known-class code pointer yields sane PIE base
cards.stop_rule: unknown-class leaks must not drive final gadget math
cards.pivot_rule: if only libc leaks exist, use them only when binary-relative closure is unnecessary
query_aliases: unknown leak cannot drive final math

cards.title: partial RELRO GOT write queue
cards.trigger: partial RELRO, GOT writable, write primitive, GOT overwrite temptation
cards.first_safe_check: prove write primitive and later consumer path before promoting GOT overwrite
cards.oracle: concrete writable GOT target and useful consumer exist, or route is demoted
cards.stop_rule: do not promote GOT overwrite to primary closure just because RELRO is partial
cards.pivot_rule: if consumer control is weak, rerank to ret2libc, output-hijack, or file-read
cards.snippet: partial RELRO GOT write enters queue only with proof

cards.title: static ORW before shell
cards.trigger: static binary, syscall heavy, seccomp, shell blocked, ORW likely
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-syscall-orw-check and one direct ORW/read-flag test
cards.oracle: direct file-read path works or outperforms shell-first attempts
cards.stop_rule: do not keep forcing shell once syscall evidence says ORW is shorter
cards.pivot_rule: if ORW cannot assemble, downgrade to the smallest reachable read/output closure
cards.snippet: static or sandbox route prefers ORW

cards.title: CXX UAF data-only first
cards.trigger: C++ wrapper objects, inventory menu, stale reference, adjacent consumer, object field reuse
cards.first_safe_check: adjacency reasoning and one focused data-only or output-hijack route
cards.oracle: shorter adjacent consumer path appears or data-only branch is falsified
cards.stop_rule: do not promote FSOP or vtable takeover before one adjacency/data-only check
cards.pivot_rule: if no shorter consumer exists, keep current heap/control route and document why
cards.snippet: C++ UAF should test data-only closure first

cards.title: Bundled libc first: lock runtime before heap probing
cards.trigger: bundled libc, bundled ld, wrong base, heap pollution
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-libc-runtime-doctor
cards.oracle: recommended runtime lock and explicit loader command appear
cards.stop_rule: do not validate heap overlap or tcache on a mismatched base once bundled libc/ld is present
cards.pivot_rule: if runtime doctor cannot be satisfied, stop heap validation and relock substrate

cards.title: Exact read(size+1) menu desync contract
cards.trigger: read(size+1), exact-length raw reads, mixed menu/raw phases
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-menu-contract-probe
cards.oracle: helper contract distinguishes exact raw read vs line-based read
cards.stop_rule: do not keep flipping sendlineafter/sendafter until helper semantics are explicit
cards.pivot_rule: if helper semantics remain unclear, spend the next probe on protocol locking

cards.title: glibc version route map
cards.trigger: glibc version, hooks, safe-linking, FILE, setcontext, version-gated heap
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-libc-resolver + ctf-pwn-libc-fingerprint
cards.oracle: version bucket and route pressure are explicit
cards.stop_rule: do not name heap techniques before version bucket is known
cards.pivot_rule: if version is unknown, relock runtime first

cards.title: glibc 2.27 fake stdout short playbook
cards.trigger: glibc 2.23-2.27, fake stdout, _IO_FILE leakage, shorter closure needed
cards.first_safe_check: runtime + leak class confirmation
cards.oracle: first leak or stable output differential appears
cards.stop_rule: rerank to setcontext+53 and direct ORW if fake stdout is slow
cards.pivot_rule: if no concrete leak appears quickly, prefer setcontext+53 ORW

cards.title: free_hook to setcontext+53 to ORW
cards.trigger: context restore route, ORW shorter than shell, writable frame exists
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-libc-resolver + frame placement proof
cards.oracle: context restore lands and ORW becomes direct
cards.stop_rule: if trigger/frame placement cannot be proven quickly, rerank to direct ORW or output-hijack
cards.pivot_rule: if hooks are removed or unreliable, demote hook route

cards.title: seccomp closure router
cards.trigger: seccomp, sandbox, syscall allowlist, blocked shell
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-syscall-orw-check
cards.oracle: allowlist and direct file-read path become explicit
cards.stop_rule: do not keep forcing shell once ORW/FILE/output-hijack is shorter
cards.pivot_rule: if syscall surface is unknown, spend next probe on syscall evidence

cards.title: Wrong libc anti-pattern for heap verification
cards.trigger: bundled libc, mismatched base, heap validation drift
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-libc-runtime-doctor
cards.oracle: runtime gate says current base is mismatched
cards.stop_rule: do not trust earlier heap observations until the runtime is relocked
cards.pivot_rule: if base is mismatched, stop heap mutation and relock

cards.title: Exact read / menu helper drift anti-pattern
cards.trigger: read(size+1), mixed menu/raw, helper pollution
cards.first_safe_check: ctf-pwn-menu-contract-probe
cards.oracle: helper contract locks line vs raw semantics
cards.stop_rule: do not keep changing sendlineafter/sendafter until helper semantics are explicit
cards.pivot_rule: if protocol is unclear, lock helper before payload mutation

cards.title: got stringified pointer leak
cards.trigger: printf/puts argument can point at GOT entry, GOT entry resolved, output treats pointer bytes as char*
cards.first_safe_check: read card trigger, generate minimal probe, confirm/falsify before closure
cards.oracle: card-specific confirm oracle
cards.stop_rule: do not promote closure before primitive probe is verified or falsified
cards.pivot_rule: pivot only on real oracle or blocker
query_aliases: primitive leak got stringified pointer leak

cards.title: got page bulk write pollutes stdio
cards.trigger: Partial RELRO or writable GOT-adjacent page., A bulk read/write starts at or near GOT/global pointer region., `.got.plt`, `.data`, `.bss`, `stdin`, `stdout`, or `stderr` are close in memory., Program crashes inside libc stdio after a large write.
cards.first_safe_check: read card trigger, generate minimal probe, confirm/falsify before closure
cards.oracle: card-specific confirm oracle
cards.stop_rule: do not promote closure before primitive probe is verified or falsified
cards.pivot_rule: pivot only on real oracle or blocker
cards.snippet: got page bulk write pollutes stdio
